rxn_class_summary: Direct electrochemical difluorination and azo-fluorination of gem-difluorostyrenes using Et3N·3HF as fluoride source to access perfluoroalkyl aromatics
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of gem-difluorostyrene to a radical cation, then a second anodic oxidation of the benzyl radical to a benzyl cation; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: benzylic radical after F- addition at C1 (II)
trifluoromethyl benzylic product after second F- addition (3)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the gem-difluorostyrene to a radical cation (I); fluoride from Et3N·3HF attacks C1 to give a benzylic radical (II); a second anodic oxidation furnishes a benzyl cation (III), which is trapped by a second fluoride (or other nucleophile such as azole/alcohol) to deliver the difunctionalized product; protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: alkyl-substituted gem-difluoroalkene (11, non-conjugated)
probe_reagents.observation: No product 12 formed, indicating a conjugated aryl group is essential to enable substrate oxidation

probe_reagents.name: controlled-potential electrolysis at 1.05 V vs SCE (below oxidation onset)
probe_reagents.observation: No product 3a at 1.05 V; 9% at 1.30 V, supporting oxidation of the gem-difluorostyrene as the initiating step

probe_reagents.name: O2 (radical trapping under O2 atmosphere)
probe_reagents.observation: Hydroperoxide/methoxy adduct 13 (MW 280.27) detected by GC-MS, consistent with a benzyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: 4-vinylbiphenyl (non-fluorinated styrene)
probe_reagents.observation: Easily oxidized but no fluorination occurs, indicating the gem-difluoro motif is required for productive F- capture

probe_reagents.name: TMSCN as competing nucleophile
probe_reagents.observation: Fluorocyanation product 8 detected by GC-MS, consistent with cation interception by CN-

notes: Mechanism additionally supported by selective cathodic hydrodefluorination/deuteration of the benzyl C-F bond of product 3g (giving 9/10 in 83%/81%), demonstrating reversible electrochemical handling of the C-F bond. No DFT or KIE studies reported.
